title: Buttery Creamy Argentinian Butter Stir-fry
description: A cozy and comforting dish featuring buttermilk, roasted vegetables, and a creamy sauce made from butter for a deliciously rich and flavorful meal.

ingredients.name: Mixed Vegetables (e.g. bell peppers, onions, carrots)

instructions: Preheat oven to 425°F (218°C). Roast garlic cloves in the oven for about 20 minutes until they are soft and golden brown.
In a bowl, mix buttermilk with roasted garlic. Add saffron threads and let it sit for at least 30 minutes.
Season chicken breasts with salt and pepper on both sides. Mix all-purpose flour with olive oil in another bowl. Dip each chicken breast into the seasoned flour mixture to coat evenly.
Heat butter in a large skillet over medium-high heat. Once melted, add the coated chicken pieces to the skillet and cook for about 5 minutes on each side or until golden brown and cooked through.
Add mixed vegetables to the same pan used for cooking chicken, stirring frequently to prevent burning. Cook for about 7-10 minutes until vegetables are tender-crisp.
In a medium bowl, stir together cream cheese with a little bit of milk until smooth. Stir in buttermilk mixture and cook over low heat for another 3-5 minutes until the sauce thickens slightly.
Serve chicken and vegetables topped with creamy sauce. Garnish with fresh herbs like parsley or cilantro if desired.
